title: Transformation of Phaeodactylum tricornutum by electroporation
author: Gina Filloramo
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.s4cegsw

steps.title: P
steps.instruction: P. tricornutum (CCMP632) cells were grown axenically in f/2 + Si medium at 22°C and illuminated with 75 μmol photons m − 2 s − 1 , 12L:12D. For growth on solid medium, cultures were grown on 50% ASW containing f/2 nutrients, 1.2% agar.

steps.title: pPha-T1/eGFP
steps.instruction: pPha-T1/eGFP : carries the bleomycin-resistant gene ( sh ble ) under the fcpB promoter and eGFP gene ( egfp ) under the fcpA promoter. Kindly provided by Peter Kroth. pPhaT1 GenBank accession : AF219942, [Zaslavskaia et al. (2000). Transformation of the diatom Phaeodactylum tricornutum (Bacillariophyceae) with a variety of selectable marker and reporter genes. Journal of Phycology , 36 , 379-386]

steps.title: Digest ~6 µg of pPha-T1/eGFP with ScaI for 2
steps.instruction: Digest ~6 µg of pPha-T1/eGFP with ScaI for 2.5 hours. Then purify linearized plasmid (i.e. Macherey-Nagel Nucleo-Spin gel and PCR clean-up kit).

steps.title: Grow P
steps.instruction: Grow P. tricornutum to a cell density of 5 × 10 6 cells/ml.

steps.title: Harvest a total of 2 ×...
steps.instruction: Harvest a total of 2 × 10 8 cells by centrifuging at 1500 g for 10 min at 4°C. Remove medium.

steps.title: Chill a 0
steps.instruction: Chill a 0.2 cm electroporation cuvette on ice.

steps.title: Wash cells three times with 1...
steps.instruction: Wash cells three times with 1 ml 375 mM sorbitol (sterile and ice cold). If needed, spin between washes at 1500 g for 1-2 mins at 4°C. Before third wash, transfer cells to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, perform final sorbitol wash and then resuspend washed cells in 100 μl 375 mM sorbitol to a final density of 2 × 10 9 cells/ml.

steps.title: Mix 100 μl aliquot of resuspended...
steps.instruction: Mix 100 μl aliquot of resuspended cells with 4 μg (0.2 μg/μl) of linearized plasmid DNA and 40 μg (10 μg/μl) of denatured salmon sperm DNA.

steps.title: Incubate cells/linearized plasmid/salmon sperm DNA mixture...
steps.instruction: Incubate cells/linearized plasmid/salmon sperm DNA mixture on ice for 10 min, then transfer to the 0.2 cm electroporation cuvette.

steps.title: Adjust Electroporation System to exponential decay...
steps.instruction: Adjust Electroporation System to exponential decay and set the following parameters: 500 V field strength 25 μF capacitance 400 Ohm shunt resistance

steps.title: After electroporation, immediately transfer cells to...
steps.instruction: After electroporation, immediately transfer cells to 15-ml conical Falcon tubes containing 10 ml f/2 medium. Allow cells to recover by incubating in low light (~ 30 μmol photons m − 2 s −1 ) for 24 hours before adding antibiotic selection. Incubate under normal culturing conditions for 24 hours then collect cells by centrifuging at 1500 g for 10 min and resuspending cells in 0.6 ml f/2 medium. Plate serial dilutions onto solid medium containing 75 μg/ml zeocin.

steps.title: Colonies should start appearring after about 12-14 days
steps.instruction: Colonies should start appearring after about 12-14 days.
